From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2016 single by Luke Bryan

| "Move" | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") by [Luke Bryan](/wiki/Luke_Bryan "Luke Bryan") | |
| from the album  *[Kill the Lights](/wiki/Kill_the_Lights_(Luke_Bryan_album) "Kill the Lights (Luke Bryan album)")* | |
| Released | July 25, 2016 (2016-07-25) |
| Recorded | 2015 |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Country rock](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") |
| Length | 3:47 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Capitol Nashville](/wiki/Capitol_Records_Nashville "Capitol Records Nashville") |

| [Luke Bryan](/wiki/Luke_Bryan "Luke Bryan") singles chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | "[Huntin', Fishin' and Lovin' Every Day](/wiki/Huntin%27,_Fishin%27_and_Lovin%27_Every_Day "Huntin', Fishin' and Lovin' Every Day")"  (2016) | "**Move**"  (2016) | "[Fast](/wiki/Fast_(Luke_Bryan_song) "Fast (Luke Bryan song)")"  (2016) | | |

"**Move**" is a song co-written and recorded by American [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") artist [Luke Bryan](/wiki/Luke_Bryan "Luke Bryan"). It was released to American country radio on July 25, 2016 as the fifth official single from his 2015 album *[Kill the Lights](/wiki/Kill_the_Lights_(Luke_Bryan_album) "Kill the Lights (Luke Bryan album)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Bryan wrote this song with [Michael Carter](/wiki/Michael_Carter_(musician) "Michael Carter (musician)") and Jay Clementi.

## Content

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Move_(Luke_Bryan_song)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Content")]

"Move" is a moderate uptempo tune that is about a Northern girl who has moved to the South, where she has "got in with some Southern belles" and her moves have become even more attractive.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Commercial performance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Move_(Luke_Bryan_song)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Commercial performance")]

The song debuted at number 43 on the US *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* [Hot Country Songs](/wiki/Hot_Country_Songs "Hot Country Songs") chart at number 43 in August 2015 and became available for download with the release of the album *[Kill the Lights](/wiki/Kill_the_Lights_(Luke_Bryan_album) "Kill the Lights (Luke Bryan album)")*,[[3]](#cite_note-3) selling 8,000 copies in its first week.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It re-entered the chart at number 42 nearly a year later after being released as a single.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The song has sold 254,000 copies in the US as of January 2017.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Music video

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Move_(Luke_Bryan_song)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Music video")]

The music video was directed by [Shane Drake](/wiki/Shane_Drake "Shane Drake") and premiered in September 2016.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The video features Luke performing in a club with his band and a female dancer dancing next door. The dancer then breaks through the wall at the final chorus and dances on stage with Luke and the band. Luke is dressed in a baseball cap and T-shirt for the duration of the video, until the final scene when he is dancing with the girl, in which he wears a black suit and white undershirt.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
